title: Editas Medicine Announces Progress Towards 2024 Goals and In Vivo Preclinical Proof of Concept
text: Editas Medicine announced significant progress towards 2024 goals including achievement of in vivo preclinical proof of concept and strategic update. The company identified two lead in vivo development candidates and achieved preclinical proof-of-concept data demonstrating efficient delivery of CRISPR machinery directly to hepatic stem/progenitor cells, enabling permanent in vivo gene editing without ex vivo cell manipulation. In vivo studies in mice demonstrated robust LDLR upregulation and over 90 percent LDL cholesterol reduction. Company also announced it initiated process to seek commercial partner or out-license reni-cel program to focus resources on in vivo pipeline. Advanced research in parallel including HBG1/2 editing for sickle cell disease and beta thalassemia in preclinical models. Expected to declare additional development candidates and establish one new target cell type/tissue by year-end 2024.
